summary:
Concomitant anterior/apital repair improves stress urinary incontinence but has no effect on the overactive bladder component; urgency and frequency remain unchanged and necessitate independent treatment with medications like anticholinergics. The surgery addresses structural issues without influencing detrusor overactivity causing urges. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$

The agent concludes that combining anterior/apical repair with midurethral slings does not enhance overactive bladder symptoms since these arise from detrusor overactivity unrelated to pelvic support repairs. Surgical intervention focuses exclusively on stress incontinence, meaning urge frequency and urgency continue to need standalone pharmaceutical therapy.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$  
Only one summary should appear at the very beginning — all other summaries have been removed per instructions.

Concomitant anterior/apical repair does not improve the overactive bladder component because it treats stress incontinence structurally, leaving urgency and detrusor overactivity unaffected and requiring separate pharmacologic management. Evidence shows no beneficial impact on urge frequency or intensity regardless of simultaneous surgical procedures targeting pelvic floor anatomy.
